## What Was Being Done
Memory saves confirmed. Both entries indexed in MEMORY.md.
**Harness audit: 0.58** — notable drops from two known issues:
- **Write Serial (23rd consecutive):** The 10 reference file Writes for AIS-23 ran sequentially despite being in one message batch. Same root cause as always — Claude Code executes tool calls within a turn serially. The rule in CLAUDE.md isn't fixable by intent alone; it requires architectural change in how the runtime parallelizes tool calls. Noted, moving on.
- **ToolSearch repeated:** Called at L2 (TodoWrite bootstrap) and L42 (close-out). The close-out ToolSearch was unnecessary — those tools (harness-audit, recall) are always the same. CLAUDE.md strengthened with explicit close-out callout.
- **17 violations:** Mostly structural Paperclip bash_grep (allowlist candidate), 2 genuine bash_ls during close-out.
**Positive:** 6th consecutive compliant session on memory saves — that pattern is fully graduated and removed from tracking.
